title: Enchanted Venezuelan Buckwheat Flatbread
description: Cultured, flaky flatbread made with buckwheat flour and miso marinate, baked on a salt block for a kid-friendly Venezuelan snack.
instructions: Whisk together miso paste, coconut oil, apple cider vinegar, lime juice, salt, cumin, turmeric, and paprika until smooth.
Add buckwheat flour and coconut milk to the miso mixture and stir until a stiff dough forms.
Cover dough with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to ferment slightly.
Release dough onto a lightly floured surface and divide into 4 equal portions.
Roll each portion into a thin circle about 8 inches in diameter, using a rolling pin and a little buckwheat flour to prevent sticking.
Preheat a salt block or a cast‑iron skillet over medium heat until shimmering.
Place one flatbread on the hot salt block and cook for 2 minutes, then flip and cook for another 1 minute until golden.
Transfer cooked flatbread to a plate and cover with a clean towel to keep warm while you cook the remaining pieces.
Serve the flatbread warm, optionally topped with chopped cilantro and a squeeze of lime for extra flavor.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days; reheat on the salt block for a few seconds to restore crispness.
